exemplar.title: How has the launch of BMW i5 model improves BMW sustainability and profitability of the company?

detailedExemplarData.title: How has the launch of BMW i5 model improves BMW sustainability and profitability of the company?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly identifies sustainability as the driving concept and maintains thematic focus throughout introduction, each analytical tool, and conclusion.
Consistently connects BMWâs i5 launch to sustainability considerations in organizational strategy and risk analysis.
weaknesses: Occasional descriptive passages (e.g., brandâreputation points) rather than deeper critical interrogation of sustainability.
Some asserted links (e.g., demand or cost claims) lack supporting data or deeper exploration of strategic objectives.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the investigation occasionally reverts to description, sustainability is woven through every sectionâtools, data analysis, and conclusionâdemonstrating a comprehensive, fully integrated approach. This merits the highest band.

strengths: Sources are generally relevant to BMWâs EV strategy and include direct industry and financial data.
Supporting documents provide sufficient context for the Triple Bottom Line and profitability analysis.
weaknesses: Seven documents exceed the recommended range, diluting focus and suggesting redundancy.
Over-reliance on a single press release limits depth; lacks primary or peer-reviewed sources.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Document relevance and depth align with mid-band expectations, but the excessive number of sources forces a reduction to band 2 to reflect both count and uneven depth.

strengths: Employs a mix of strategic (Ansoff), sustainability (Triple Bottom Line) and financial ratio analysis tools.
Each tool is directly applied to the research question on sustainability and profitability.
weaknesses: Applications often remain descriptive and occasionally speculative (e.g., attributing margin shifts solely to the i5).
Methodology lacks clarity on data selection and rationale for analysis periods.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection is adequate and relevant, and tools are applied in line with the research question, matching the criteria for band 3.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses quarterly gross and net profit tables and charts to ground analysis in empirical data.
Draws connections between launch timing and profit trends, offering moments of insight into causality and risk.
weaknesses: Analysis is patchy with assumed causality and limited sustainability metrics.
Evaluation of riskâreward trade-offs (e.g., competitor response, market saturation) is underdeveloped.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection is adequate and the analysis moves beyond mere description, fitting the requirements for band 3 but lacking sustained, mostly effective integration for a higher band.

strengths: Clearly restates that the i5 launch improved both sustainability and profitability, directly answering the research question.
Conclusions align in part with the findings presented in the analysis.
weaknesses: Contains unqualified assertions (e.g., causes of margin improvement) without triangulated evidence.
Lacks synthesis across tools and actionable recommendations, and often repeats earlier points.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are consistent with evidence and address the research question, but are only partly grounded and insufficiently synthesized to justify the top band.

strengths: Logical division into sections with a contents page, headings, and clear overall flow.
Appropriate structure supports reader navigation throughout the report.
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers and formatting is inconsistent.
Some headings mix analysis and conclusion, causing minor clarity issues.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure is coherent and appropriate throughout, meeting the criteria for the highest band in this category.

strengths: All required IB elements are present: title, word count, contents, diagrams, citations, and bibliography.
Consistent inclusion of diagrams and footnotes shows attention to presentation requirements.
weaknesses: Footnote superscripts are misformatted (<math> tags) and citation styles vary across entries.
Diagram numbering and labels are inconsistent, and there are typographical errors (e.g., â15 Modelâ instead of âi5â).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite formatting inconsistencies, all required elements are included and the report meets the highest bandâs standards for presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report is divided into logical sections with a contents page, headings and clear flow, meeting expectations for âappropriate structure throughoutâ. Minor inconsistencies in sub-heading numbering do not undermine overall organisation.

Most sources are press releases, industry articles or financial databases directly related to BMW or the EV sector, so relevance is solid. However, depth is uneven: primary data beyond profit margins is lacking and some citations repeat the same BMW press release. Hence documents are ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ but not wide-ranging enough for the top band.
Title, word count, table of contents, diagrams, citations and bibliography are present. All required IB elements appear to be included, so the highest presentation band is appropriate.

Seven supporting documents are listed, exceeding the recommended 3â5. According to the rubric, providing more than five documents falls into the same category as supplying only one or two, so the strand must be placed in the second band (1 mark).

Each tool is applied directly to the research question: TBL explores sustainability effects, Ansoff reviews growth strategy, and margin analysis assesses profitability. Relevance is clear but application remains descriptive and sometimes speculative (e.g., attributing margin changes solely to the i5). This fits ârelevance not always clear but adequately appliedâ, the third band.

The student selects quarterly gross and net profit data for 2023â24, uses them in a table and chart, and attempts comparison before and after launch. While additional operational or sustainability metrics would strengthen the case, the data choice is adequate, meeting band-3 expectations for âadequate selection and useâ.

Analysis moves beyond descriptionâlinking profit trends to launch timing, competitive risk and TBL factorsâbut integration is patchy and causality is sometimes assumed rather than evidenced. This demonstrates âsome analysisâ with moments of insight, aligning with the third band; it stops short of âmostly effectiveâ sustained integration required for band-4.
The student explicitly restates that the launch improved both sustainability and profitability, directly answering the research question in the final paragraph, satisfying band-3 requirements.

The IA employs three toolsâthe Triple Bottom Line, Ansoff Matrix and rudimentary financial ratio analysis. This represents an âadequate selectionâ covering strategic, sustainability and financial perspectives, though breadth is limited and no alternative strategic frameworks or qualitative research tools are used, preventing placement in the highest band.

Conclusions broadly match earlier findings yet contain unqualified assertions (e.g., margin improvement is âdue toâ eco-conscious demand) without triangulated evidence. They are partly but not wholly grounded in the presented analysis, thus âsome conclusions consistent with evidenceâ (band-2).

BMW and the i5 launch are at the centre of every section. The student cites company strategy documents, profit figures and competitive context, showing a clear, organisation-specific focus. Nevertheless, integration is not seamless: some claims about demand or cost are asserted with limited supporting data, and links back to BMWâs wider strategic objectives are only partially explored. This reflects âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ rather than the comprehensive, throughout-assessment integration demanded by the highest band.

The student consistently frames the investigation around the key concept of sustainability, weaving references to environmental and social responsibility through the introduction, each analytical tool and the conclusion. While the concept clearly guides the choice of Triple Bottom Line and the commentary on product development risk, the discussion sometimes slips into description (e.g., repeated brandâreputation points) rather than deep, critical integration. Overall, the concept underpins analysis but is not exhaustively interrogated, aligning with âstrong understanding with partially-integrated analysisâ rather than the fully-integrated standard required for the top band.

annotations.comment: The Ansoff discussion catalogues product development but omits any critique of associated riskâreward trade-offs. Include evaluation of competitor responses and market saturation risk.
The student clearly identifies âSUSTAINABILITYâ as the key concept, setting a strong thematic foundation for the investigation.
The research questionâs grammar is awkward. Revise to something like: âHow has the launch of the BMW i5 model improved BMWâs sustainability and profitability?â
Table of contents entries lack page numbers and exhibit inconsistent heading formatting. Include precise page references for clarity.
This paragraph ends mid-sentence, leaving the reader without closure. Complete the thought and ensure each idea is fully articulated.
The introduction describes BMWâs offerings but does not explicitly link these details back to the stated research question on sustainability and profitability.
The rewrite of the research question on page 4 is repetitive and wordy. Streamline to avoid redundancy and focus on analytical framing.
The Triple Bottom Line section outlines the 3Ps but remains largely descriptive. Add deeper analysis of how each âPâ specifically impacts BMWâs strategic objectives.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The social impact statement trails off: âimproves their social responsibility and reputation of theââcomplete this thought and provide specific evidence.
Numerous footnote superscripts appear as math tags (e.g., <math> tags). Standardize superscript formatting to maintain professional presentation.
The economic impact discussion is broad and lacks any quantitative data. Incorporate specific sales or margin figures to substantiate claims.
This paragraph blends analysis with conclusion without clear separation, making it hard to follow which are findings versus final judgments.
The link between innovation costs and enhanced profitability is asserted without data. Cite relevant margins or cost figures to validate this claim.
Figure labels are inconsistent: the text calls it âDiagram 2.0â but the earlier profit margin chart is âDiagram 3.0.â Ensure a unified numbering scheme.
The conclusion of the financial analysis attributes profitability changes solely to the i5 launch without considering other market factors. Broaden evaluation.
The main conclusion reiterates tool-by-tool findings but stops short of synthesizing insights or proposing actionable recommendations.
This section over-relies on Supporting Document 2, limiting depth of perspectives. Diversify sources to include primary data or peer-reviewed analysis.
Typographical error: the text reads â15 Modelâ instead of âi5 Model.â Ensure consistent naming of the product throughout.
Table 1.0 presents quarterly gross and net margins clearly, providing a solid empirical basis for analysis.
Methodology lacks clarity: describe how profit margins were calculated and why these quarters were chosen to isolate the i5 launch effect.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The final paragraph on page 15 largely repeats earlier conclusions. Enhance by integrating across tools and linking clearly back to the research question.
Seven supporting documents are listed, exceeding the recommended 3â5 range. Prioritize the most relevant sources and remove redundant ones.
Citation formatting varies across entries (e.g., inconsistent author/date styles). Standardize to a single academic referencing style.
Diagram 1.0 lacks labels for specific Triple Bottom Line dimensions, and the narrative does not reference individual components. Link diagram and text explicitly.
Within page 6 the header â[SUPPORTING DOCUMENT 2]â disrupts the flow of analysis. Integrate source details in footnotes or bibliography instead.
The claim of âincoming orders worldwideâ would be stronger with actual order volume or pre-sales figures rather than an unquantified assertion.
